TECHNICAL ANALYSIS

Advanced Knowledge

Smart Money & Dumb Money, The Never-Ending Battle

💡 Little premise:

The content I'm elaborating with my “personality” is the fruit of the knowledge spread by Chris Lori and Michael Huddlestone, ICT (Inner Circle Trader) and I want to share it with you because it's a highly valuable content. It’s valuable because it debunked all of my beliefs in financial markets and actually helped me deliver the proper mindset when approaching them. This space is a jungle and as such, you need to know what’s truly behind it. I'm not “inventing” anything, the whole concept belongs to them, who have interpreted the Wyckoff scheme re-structuring it with these inputs. I'm simply re-elaborating it with “crypto vibes”.

1. Mental Paradigm

Let's start with a big assumption: if you’re here and you’re passionate about financial markets, it’s because you want to improve your life. This means you’re the living proof that more and more people are approaching this world. During the Covid pandemic in fact, mass boredom has created a huge “trading phenomenon” with millions of individuals throwing money into financial markets. The main problem is that these people approach such a complicated world unaware of what’s truly behind it.

Most, if not everyone (me included years ago) started investing/trading with the idea that this space will provide “easy money”.

“This year I will quit my job and make millions, it’s so obvious!”

This is just one of the multitude of bullshits that we like to tell ourselves. Very often this is nourished by the wrong idea that certain people promote. Traders with Lambos, Domperignons, hot girls on their sides, fancy houses and big piles of money “sit” on the car seat. These are the classic pics that leverage people’s emotions chasing the dream of pressing a few buttons while drinking a mojito on top of the highest pool of Dubai. This illusion triggers some sort of inner motivation, forcing to catch trades and rush into financial markets, starting with the wrong mental paradigm.

What 99% of people ignore, is not the technicalities needed to succeed in the market but is against who they’re gonna play against. Because trading and investing are the 2 most competitive disciplines in the world. People who enter financial markets don't understand they’re gonna compete against the big banks and trust me, they're not here for charity purposes. They’re involved in the markets to make hundreds of millions.

2. You thought it was a fair game?

“Manipulation? Nah, mate it’s all conspiracy theories.”

This is what I generally hear from non-professional guys. But of course, I'm here to prove to you that they’re wrong. Look at this article, via 2 cartels, 5 banks (Barclays, JPMorgan, Royal Bank Of Scotland, Citigroup and MUFG) manipulated the forex market, between 2007 and 2013, using chat rooms to share information regarding orders, prices, trading activities

I don't know if you properly understood the magnitude. They were sharing insightful information between them regarding retail’s trading activities. Like watching at the market with X-rays already knowing where retail traders put stop losses, market buys, selling targets and so on. The passe-partout for making money.

This happened in a regulated market such as the Forex one, what do you think happens in the crypto land where the average guy thinks he can become rich overnight with a few bucks? Yes, you got it: it’s even worse and the roots of manipulation are dangerously solid. This should trigger a big warning in your mind.

3. Smart Money VS Dumb Money

I'm sure you heard about these 2 factions plenty of times during your financial journey. But what are they, in reality?

Smart money (SM) are essentially Banks, Investment Funds, Venture Capitalists, Exchanges, Institutional Investors and very few selected people like experienced traders, for example. These are the ones who win.

Dumb money (DM) are instead retail traders, people who go leverage with all the funds and all the individuals who panic buy or panic sell a financial asset. Not-informed money which, statistically speaking, constitutes 80/90% of the money present in the market. And these are the ones who lose.

So why is this relevant, you might be asking. We all know that institutions and banks operate in niches like stocks, commodities, forex and other traditional finance sectors. But given the strong “TradFi” evolution that the crypto world is embracing (KYC everywhere, Binance collaborating with banks, etc) it is logical to assume that smart money are here in full force. This, again, should trigger a warning : every time we open a position, we’re trading AGAINST them.

We’re trading against entities that spend their entire life studying financial markets and actually invest billions into knowledge and working systems. The difference here is that Smart Money have a deep knowledge of how Dumb money work while the last one don't know which is the concept of Smart Money. Smart Money know that Dumb Money uses classic trading indicators in the wrong way (buy when RSI is low and sell when RSI is high, for example) and they also know that people rely on emotions to make their financial decisions. That's why they win.

The equation, is very simple: For each buyer there must be a seller. For each person who makes a profit, there must be a person who loses.

This whole mechanism contributes to creating liquidity, the key concept this content revolves around and the magnet for every financial market. A market is defined liquid when the assets tradable in that specific market could be exchanged quickly. This means that when you buy or sell, there will be instantly the counterpart that will satisfy your needs.

4. The chase is on

We’ve seen that smart money operates behind the scenes, but we didn't see how. Imagine a big hunting season where these entities go to catch their prey. But who are the prey? Retails, you might be thinking, and this is correct. But the appropriate expression for prey is liquidity. So how do they do? To understand it, we first need to cover the difference between 2 trading styles.

  •  Discretionary Trading:

Discretionary trading is a type of trading where the trader makes decisions on buying and selling based on their own judgment, experience, and analysis of the market.

  • Algorithmic trading:

In algorithmic trading instead, computer programs or algorithms are used to execute trades based on predefined criteria such as price, timing, quantity, or mathematical models.

The last is the one used by SM as it removes the emotional part, and it’s also the one in which smart money invest the most capital. And the majority of algorithms work on “breakout” strategies. A breakout strategy works by executing trades when asset prices surpass predetermined support or resistance levels. This strategy simply capitalizes on the influx of buy and sell orders from both retail traders and algorithms at these critical levels, aiming to capture liquidity concentrated within those areas. The example below will clarify the concept a little bit more.

On the left we have a clear bullish structure, followed by a distribution phase and then the classic mark-down (bearish structure). Either traders want to ride the bullish structure (opening a long) or the bearish one (opening a short) they put orders into swing points.

Orders, concretely speaking, produce liquidity and this brings us back to the previous concept: Smart money manipulate the market to reach important HTF points, grabbing liquidity and making retail traders poor.

The more orders ~> the more liquidity

The more liquidity ~> the more potential profits for smart money

Understand why they invest so big in algorithms?

5. Example

Watch this Bitcoin weekly chart. All the wicks that have occurred caught liquidity at HTF levels.

Look at the long wick down from 50K to 42K. Market makers have pumped Bitcoin after the first wave of bear market to induce people to believe it was “just a dip” to make them rush again, taking the liquidity at 48K and then reversing it back down.

Was it a coincidence that the last wick during the January ETF has pushed exactly at 48K? No, there were historical orders in that zone. That's why I always say that wicks are just manipulative moves made by smart money.

Final Conclusion

It's not easy to deal with smart money, and that's something you probably understood reading this article. So what should be our goal, regardless if we are long term investors or traders? A main one: Understanding where the orders are to pinpoint where a liquidity drawdown is more likely to occur, giving us an edge to sell/buy at HTF levels.

So how can we do it ? Auction Market Theory | Imbalances | Fair Value | Volume Profile delves in a detailed manner into the countermeasures to adopt and brings the probabilities in our favour. One last thing to close this content. Plenty of times you saw me talking about news that match HTF levels.. Don't let anyone tell you that this market is fair, pure and completely decentralized. These are fairytales I believed in too long time ago. Fairytales created ad-hoc to keep retails inside the market and milk them until the last “drop”. It's all premeditated and manipulated. But this doesn't mean we can't make profits, right? This simply means that we have to play carefully, with cold mind and a warm heart.
